multi-table-sql.title: Multi-Table SQL & Joins
multi-table-sql.description: Move beyond single-table reports by following keys across related tables. Build INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN reports, travel through bridge tables, preserve unmatched rows, count relationships accurately, and complete a school participation capstone.
prerequisites: SQL Analysis & Reporting, or equivalent experience building clear single-table detail reports and grouped summaries.

multi-table-sql-module-0-join-foundation.title: Join Foundations
multi-table-sql-module-0-join-foundation.description: Read primary-key and foreign-key relationships, then build clear two-table INNER JOIN reports.
outcomes: Trace primary-key and foreign-key relationships in the ERD.
Use INNER JOIN and ON to connect matching rows.
Qualify columns when multiple tables contain similar names.
Use concise table aliases without hiding the relationship logic.
why: Read primary-key and foreign-key relationships, then build clear two-table INNER JOIN reports.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

multi-table-sql-module-1-relationship-pa.title: Following Relationship Paths
multi-table-sql-module-1-relationship-pa.description: Follow multi-step relationship paths through bridge tables while preserving the intended result grain.
outcomes: Distinguish one-to-many and many-to-many result behavior.
Use enrollments as a bridge between students and courses.
Extend a valid join path one relationship at a time.
Detect missing, reversed, or unrelated ON conditions.
why: Follow multi-step relationship paths through bridge tables while preserving the intended result grain.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

multi-table-sql-module-2-outer-joins-and.title: Outer Joins and Accurate Counts
multi-table-sql-module-2-outer-joins-and.description: Use LEFT JOIN to preserve unmatched rows and count related records without accidental inflation.
outcomes: Use LEFT JOIN to preserve every row from the chosen starting table.
Explain how WHERE can accidentally remove unmatched LEFT JOIN rows.
Find entities with no related records using an anti-join pattern.
Use COUNT(right_id) and COUNT(DISTINCT ...) at the intended result grain.
why: Use LEFT JOIN to preserve unmatched rows and count related records without accidental inflation.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

multi-table-sql-module-3-final-capstone.title: Final Capstone
multi-table-sql-module-3-final-capstone.description: Combine joins, aliases, grouped counts, unmatched-row handling, and deterministic ordering in a final participation report.
outcomes: Plan the full students-to-departments relationship path from a reporting brief.
Preserve every student while joining optional enrollment, course, and department rows.
Calculate enrollment, distinct-course, and distinct-department counts at one-row-per-student grain.
Add a clear participation label and deterministic counseling-priority ranking.
why: Combine joins, aliases, grouped counts, unmatched-row handling, and deterministic ordering in a final participation report.
The final work demonstrates the complete course workflow.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Relationship Keys
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Use the schema and ERD to identify which columns can connect tables.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Readable Join Queries
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Keep joined queries unambiguous as more columns appear.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Module Project
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Build one dependable two-table roster without discarding earlier work.
bullets: Module 0 Project: Student Enrollment Activity Roster

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Relationship Shapes
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Understand how relationship cardinality changes the result rows.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Longer Join Paths
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Extend and debug longer relational queries.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Module Project
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Build one four-table directory by extending a correct relationship path.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Preserving Unmatched Rows
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Use LEFT JOIN intentionally and keep its unmatched rows visible.
bullets: Filtering LEFT JOINs with ON and WHERE

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Missing Relationships and Counts
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Turn unmatched rows into useful audits and reliable grouped metrics.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Module Project
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Build one all-department scorecard with trustworthy relationship counts.

multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.title: Final Capstone
multi-table-sql-multi-table-sql-section-.description: Complete one focused multi-table report from brief to final validated query.
bullets: Final Capstone: Student Program Participation Audit
